questions.question: What is the primary goal of a deep learning network for the classification of textual documents?
options: A) Regression
B) Clustering
C) Classification
D) Dimensionality Reduction
questions.explanation: The primary goal of a deep learning network for the classification of textual documents is to predict one or more categories or classes that the document belongs to.

questions.question: Which type of deep learning network is commonly used for text classification tasks?
options: A) Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)
B) Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs)
C) Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) Networks
D) Transformers
questions.explanation: Transformers are a type of deep learning network that are specifically designed for natural language processing tasks, including text classification.

questions.question: What is the benefit of using word embeddings in text classification?
options: A) Reduced dimensionality of the feature space
B) Improved interpretability of the model
C) Enhanced ability to capture contextual relationships between words
D) Increased training speed
questions.explanation: Word embeddings represent words as vectors in a high-dimensional space, allowing the model to capture contextual relationships between words and improve its performance on text classification tasks.

questions.question: How can you prevent overfitting in a deep learning network for text classification?
options: A) Using a smaller batch size
B) Reducing the number of hidden layers
C) Regularization techniques such as dropout and L1/L2 regularization
D) Increasing the complexity of the network
questions.explanation: Regularization techniques such as dropout and L1/L2 regularization can help prevent overfitting in deep learning networks by reducing the complexity of the model and preventing it from overfitting to the training data.

questions.question: What is the role of pre-training in a deep learning network for text classification?
options: A) To fine-tune the model on a specific task
B) To transfer knowledge from one task to another
C) To reduce the size of the vocabulary
D) To improve the interpretability of the model
questions.explanation: Pre-training a deep learning network on a large corpus of text can help transfer knowledge from one task to another, improving the model's performance on the specific task at hand.
